categories.summary:
The main technical discussion centered around igor.peregudov's demonstration of the @elisym/plugin-elizaos-elisym plugin, which transforms Eliza agents into paid service providers. The plugin enables agent-to-agent transactions using a Nostr-based discovery layer for capability announcement and Solana for payment settlement. The demo showed a complete flow: an Eliza agent publishes its capabilities to Nostr, receives a paid request from Claude desktop for trending GitHub repos, settles payment on Solana devnet, and delivers the service. This creates a decentralized marketplace where agents can monetize their capabilities without intermediary platforms.

A significant discussion emerged around ELIZAOS token utility. quanteliza proposed a v3 feature allowing protocol fees to be paid in either USDC or ELIZAOS tokens, with discounts for token payments and automatic buyback/burn mechanisms for USDC payments, similar to early BNB tokenomics. This would create economic demand while maintaining the open-source framework. odilitime responded positively, stating this motivated him to complete his November work on enabling plugins to accept $elizaOS and $DegenAI as x402 payment, identifying this as the lowest-hanging fruit for token utility implementation.

Odilitime also announced updates to working groups after discussions with Shaw, and made steering bumps to key community members. There was brief discussion about Discord role management and layout feedback requests, though no specific feedback was provided in this segment.

faqs.question: Is there any way that the utility of the elisaos coin is connected to elisaos infrastructure?
What if v3 let you pay protocol fees in USDC or ELIZAOS, with a discount for paying in token + backend buyback/burn on USDC payments?

helpInteractions.context: Demonstrating how to implement and use the elisym plugin for monetizing Eliza agents
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided complete demo video, code repository, and example agent showing full agent-to-agent payment flow using Nostr discovery and Solana settlement

actionItems.description: Complete November work on enabling plugins to accept $elizaOS and $DegenAI as x402 payment
Update working groups following discussion with Shaw
Implement v3 protocol fees payable in USDC or ELIZAOS with token payment discounts and USDC buyback/burn mechanism
Configure auto-select functionality for 'I want to help' Discord role

The conversation was otherwise dominated by greetings and brief social interactions, with carissabowersei_91078 offering assistance, and roadragedave greeting thinnnn101_101. helpInteractions.context: Offered general assistance to channel members
helpInteractions.resolution: Offered help but no specific issue was addressed

categories.summary: The discussion centered on Hierarchical Task Networks (HTN) implementation in ElizaOS. thirti.eth inquired about building with alpha code and shared Claude's suggestion about upgrading HTN-lite to full HTN when Eliza v2 reaches beta, replacing hand-coded decomposition with LLM planning while maintaining existing triggers and steps. odilitime clarified HTN as a multistep approach. thirti.eth provided technical context explaining HTN as an AI planning technique from the 1970s used in game AI (F.E.A.R., Total War) and robotics, noting ElizaOS v2 added it for agent goal decomposition, though questioned the accuracy of this information. uplink2501 drew comparisons to Hierarchical Temporal Memory (HTM) and LISP. The conversation revealed uncertainty about HTN implementation details in ElizaOS v2 and its relationship to existing planning techniques.

faqs.question: Is it true that ElizaOS v2 added HTN for agent goal decomposition?
Any similarity to Hierarchical Temporal Memory (HTM)? Or more like LISP?

helpInteractions.context: odilitime asked what HTN meant in the context of ElizaOS development
helpInteractions.resolution: thirti.eth explained HTN as Hierarchical Task Networks, an AI planning technique from the 1970s used in game AI and robotics, and its purported use in ElizaOS v2 for agent goal decomposition

actionItems.description: Upgrade HTN-lite to full HTN when Eliza v2 hits beta by replacing hand-coded decomposition with LLM planning
Verify and document whether ElizaOS v2 actually includes HTN for agent goal decomposition

categories.summary: The discussion centered on token utility strategy for Eliza, with shawmakesmagic explaining the challenge of implementing token-specific utility without creating user friction. The preferred approach is to accept any payment method users have and use revenue to buy back tokens. Key development updates include v3 nearing completion, an app in final stages, and Eliza Cloud actively taking business with direct app integration. A technical issue was reported regarding the Milady Play Store link from GitHub being non-functional. The team confirmed degenai updates are imminent. The conversation also included community banter about price movements and milestone-based commitments.

faqs.question: Does the Milady Play Store link from GitHub work?
Can we expect updates about degenai soon?

actionItems.description: Fix non-functional Milady Play Store link in GitHub repository
Complete and release v3 of Eliza
Finalize and launch the Eliza app with Eliza Cloud integration
Release degenai updates

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment shows minimal technical discussion, with most content consisting of price concerns, greetings, and off-topic messages. The few technical elements include: odilitime sharing a Hyperscape Discord invite link and mentioning an 'eliza army' steering group for community coordination. A historical discussion emerged about the original 1966 ELIZA chatbot, with odilitime providing educational resources about the ELIZA effect. One user (luminousda) reported issues with Eliza but the problem appeared to resolve itself. A scammer was identified and banned by odilitime. The chat was dominated by token price complaints, with users expressing losses and requesting market-making partnerships with VCs. One user offered development services for AI and full-stack systems. Overall, this segment lacked substantive technical discussions, problem-solving, or implementation details.

categories.summary: Shaw announced that Eliza v3 and Milady integration are nearing completion, with an exciting couple of weeks ahead. He explained his preference for working in quieter development-focused servers to avoid token price discussions and maintain focus on technical work. A significant technical update was revealed: $ELIZA (elizaOS token) is now the default payment method in x402 for elizaOS/Milady services, providing concrete utility to the token. Community members provided feedback on project direction, with zadayos and cryptologos_00014 emphasizing the importance of balancing technical development with community engagement, PR planning, and business strategy. They suggested connecting the token with infrastructure utility, increasing communication frequency, and maintaining focus on relevant topics. The discussion highlighted tension between pure technical development and community/business needs. Kevin111s introduced himself as a software engineer seeking collaboration, specifically looking for a US-based partner to handle client-facing communication while he focuses on building production-ready AI workflows and backend architecture. The community welcomed new members and showed continued support for the project despite concerns about communication gaps.

faqs.question: Does A mean the ability to bill x402 services with $ELIZA?
Can we expect some update for $degenai this month?

helpInteractions.context: Clarification needed on whether $ELIZA token can be used to bill x402 services
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed that $elizaOS is the default payment in x402 for elizaOS/Milady

actionItems.description: Complete Eliza v3 and Milady integration

Connect the coin with infrastructure utility
Develop PR plan and business plan for project expansion

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment from the #💬-coders channel contains minimal technical content. The conversation consists of three brief messages: odilitime addressing a scammer, _danoski greeting the channel, and odilitime responding with a greeting. There are no technical discussions, problem-solving activities, code implementations, or development decisions present in this segment. No meaningful technical exchanges occurred during this time period.

Shaw (shawmakesmagic) provided a significant project update addressing community concerns about development progress and token performance. He clarified that Spartan provides regular updates every few hours and emphasized the team's commitment to building despite market conditions. Shaw outlined ongoing work on multiple fronts: Eliza Cloud, framework improvements, an autonomous agent with comprehensive crypto features, and alternative monetization strategies. He mentioned a token buyback strategy contingent on profitability and hinted at an upcoming 'round two.' Shaw acknowledged the challenge of making public promises in the current environment where statements can be used against builders. The discussion reflected broader market downturn concerns, with Solana's price decline mentioned as context. Community members baogerbao and zadayos expressed continued support, with baogerbao highlighting Shaw's open-source approach and community engagement, including attending BNB events in Hong Kong and creating merchandise. The team's commitment to building during the downturn was emphasized as a differentiator. Some community skepticism emerged regarding potential exchange delistings and project viability, though supporters countered with affirmations of ongoing development. Two developers (aiden190157 and rsn6958) expressed interest in contributing, with rsn6958 providing detailed credentials in AI and full-stack development focused on production-ready systems.

actionItems.description: Continue development of Eliza Cloud platform
Build autonomous agent with comprehensive crypto features
Implement token buyback strategy when project becomes profitable
Develop alternative monetization strategies for the platform

categories.summary: The discussion centered on ElizaOS v3 development and community dynamics. Shaw (shawmakesmagic) addressed community complaints and outlined v3's architecture, which moves everything into a full application runtime supporting all devices and platforms. Key features include: integration with all social platforms including iMessage, task issuance to Codex and Claude, subscription management, workflow creation capabilities, and runtime application creation/editing/execution. The system enables monetized app creation through Eliza Cloud. Shaw emphasized 6 months of continuous development with cozy devs, noting the team received no equity due to token value collapse. He clarified that ai16z and elizaos tokens provided only basic salary, and v3 is being built on personal savings. The botdick agent was showcased as an example, having created its own video game. Shaw rejected traditional token utility approaches, advocating for culture-building over forced utility mechanisms. He explained past challenges including lawsuit threats, migration issues, and team equity problems when tokens went to zero. The core value proposition is agents that can build anything users want with monetization capabilities. A new developer (0xtdl01) joined discussing local LLM data storage, agent key security, and red team swarm testing.

faqs.question: Which agent is the best in the world?
What makes Eliza v3 unique architecturally?
What is the token utility for elizaos?
Where can I see examples in addition to the docs section?

helpInteractions.context: User needed to find examples beyond documentation
